project_title: MovieDB Data Cleaning and Visualization Project

visualizations.title: Missing Values Before Cleaning
visualizations.interpretation: The raw movie dataset has a small number of missing values in title, overview, language, genre, poster URL, and numeric rating fields. These must be handled before analysis.

visualizations.title: Outlier Detection Before Treatment
visualizations.interpretation: Popularity and vote count contain very large blockbuster values. IQR capping creates stable analysis columns while preserving original values separately.

visualizations.title: Top 10 Movies by Popularity
visualizations.interpretation: The most popular movies are the highest-attention titles in the dataset. These films often represent strong public interest or platform visibility.

visualizations.title: Top 10 Movies by Vote Count
visualizations.interpretation: Vote count shows audience engagement. Movies with many votes have stronger evidence behind their rating than movies with only a few votes.

visualizations.title: Top Rated Movies with High Vote Count
visualizations.interpretation: Filtering by vote count avoids ranking movies highly just because very few people rated them. This produces a more reliable top-rated list.

visualizations.title: Top 15 Genres by Movie Count
visualizations.interpretation: Genre count shows which types of movies appear most often. Drama, comedy, action, and thriller-type genres often dominate large movie databases.

visualizations.title: Average Popularity by Genre
visualizations.interpretation: This chart shows which genres attract higher average attention. It is useful for understanding audience demand by content type.

visualizations.title: Top Languages by Movie Count
visualizations.interpretation: Language distribution shows dataset coverage. English movies dominate, while Japanese, Spanish, French, Korean, and other languages add international variety.

visualizations.title: Movie Releases by Year
visualizations.interpretation: Release trends show how the dataset changes over time. Recent years contain many movies because modern movie databases usually have richer coverage.

visualizations.title: Movie Count by Decade
visualizations.interpretation: Decade analysis summarizes long-term coverage. It helps compare older cinema representation with modern releases.

visualizations.title: Rating Distribution
visualizations.interpretation: Most movies cluster around middle-to-good ratings. Very low and perfect ratings are less common after cleaning.

visualizations.title: Popularity Distribution
visualizations.interpretation: Most movies have modest popularity, while a smaller group receives much higher attention. Capping makes the distribution easier to study.

visualizations.title: Vote Count vs Popularity
visualizations.interpretation: Popular movies often receive more votes, but the relationship is not perfect. Some movies are highly rated or voted without being the most popular at the moment.

visualizations.title: Overview Length vs Rating
visualizations.interpretation: Overview length does not strongly determine rating, but this chart checks whether richer descriptions are associated with different rating patterns.

visualizations.title: Rating Category Share
visualizations.interpretation: The pie chart groups movies into simple rating bands. This makes it easy to explain the overall quality mix of the dataset.

visualizations.title: Rating by Primary Genre
visualizations.interpretation: Boxplots compare rating ranges across genres. They show whether some genres tend to have higher median ratings or more variation.

visualizations.title: Correlation Heatmap
visualizations.interpretation: The heatmap shows relationships between numeric features. Popularity and vote count usually move together, while rating behaves differently.

visualizations.title: Pairplot of Key Movie Metrics
visualizations.interpretation: The pairplot gives a compact view of multiple relationships at once. It is useful for spotting clusters, patterns, and unusual movies.
